name: Southport

seo.description: Plan Southport with city facts, weather, sights, stays and events in England.

seo.webpageDescription: Plan Southport with city facts, weather, sights, stays and events in England.

heroText: Southport is a coastal destination in England, useful for event-led trips, seaside stays and regional travel planning.

highlights.name: Southport
highlights.text: Southport is a coastal destination in England, useful for event-led trips, seaside stays and regional travel planning.

history.label: 18th century
history.text: The town gets its name from the South Port Hotel; built around 1797 (see #18th century). 10thÃ¢â‚¬â€œ17th century There have been settlements in the area now comprising Southport since the Domesday Book, and some.
In 1792, William Sutton, the landlord of the Black Bull Inn in Churchtown (now the Hesketh Arms) and known to locals as "The Old Duke", realised the importance of the newly created canal systems.
When a widow from Wigan built a cottage nearby in 1797 for seasonal lodgers, Sutton quickly built a new inn on the site of the bathing house which he called the South Port Hotel.
A design from James Brunlees was approved at a cost of Ã‚Â£8,700 and on 4 August 1859 a large crowd witnessed the driving home of the first support pile.
On the night of 9 December 1886, the worst lifeboat disaster in the history of the UK occurred off the shores of Southport.

history.label: 20th century
history.text: Mexico was just one of many shipwrecks in the Southport area. 20th century From 1894 to 1912 Birkdale and the adjoining village of Ainsdale were separate from Southport and administered by Birkdale Urban District.

In 1914, a very short romance story between a "2 park road Southport" private soldier and French lady took place in Valenciennes in north France during early First World War as described by AndrÃƒÂ©e.
In 1925, the RNLI abandoned the station at Southport and left the town with no lifeboat.
On 16 March 1926, Henry Segrave set the land speed record in his 4-litre Sunbeam Tiger Ladybird on the sands at Southport at 152.33 mph (245.15 km/h).
Under the Local Government Act 1972, Southport was duly included in the Metropolitan Borough of Sefton, which was established on 1 April 1974.

history.label: 21st century
history.text: Parry-Thomas. 21st century Southport elected their first ever Labour MP in the 2024 general election.

In 2002, a local independent party calling themselves the Southport Party was established, with many members supporting a policy of "Southport out of Sefton." Three council seats were won in the 2002 local elections.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: There was no port, but "Southport" soon became the name of the town.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Other seaside bathing areas could not really get going until the railways were built some years later.
